ideas.title: The Claude Small-Business Agent Actually Doing the Work
ideas.evidence: Braydon’s May 18 Claude small-business agent post is already at 12.1K views and 161 shares. That is the strongest new own-account signal from the current scrape.
ideas.onScreenTitle: “I gave Claude one boring business task”
ideas.spokenHook: “This is what Claude’s new small-business agent actually does when you give it a real job.”
ideas.why: The audience already responded to the announcement. The V2 earns its slot by moving from “new agent exists” to “here is the task it can actually take off your plate.”

ideas.title: Ask Better Finance Questions Before You Call Your Accountant
ideas.evidence: ChatGPTapp posted the finance preview on May 16, and the earlier finance/accounting competitor signal from nocode.joshua remains a high-performing category signal. The current X scan also showed Sam Altman carrying the ChatGPT product momentum forward on May 18.
ideas.onScreenTitle: “Before you call your accountant, ask ChatGPT this”
ideas.spokenHook: “Do not let AI make your money decisions. But it can help you ask way better questions.”
ideas.why: It gives Frank a clear use case in 5 seconds and avoids the replacement framing. It also ties a real OpenAI product update to a normal business owner problem.

ideas.title: Claude Plus Canva, But Make It a Client Deliverable
ideas.evidence: Sabrina has two current Claude + Canva posts at 58.1K and 31.9K views. Braydon’s own Canva post is at 9.29K views. The source pattern is live, but Braydon’s angle should be more practical and less “Canva got replaced.”
ideas.onScreenTitle: “Claude made this client flyer in Canva”
ideas.spokenHook: “If you run a small business, this is the part of AI that actually saves you time.”
ideas.why: It borrows the live Canva wave without copying the competitor framing. Braydon’s distinct angle is the business deliverable, not the tool drama.

ideas.title: Google I/O Watchlist, But Only If the Demo Is Visual
ideas.evidence: GoogleDeepMind posted on May 18 that Google I/O begins May 19 at 10 AM PT with AI breakthroughs and tools. This is fresh, but it has not earned a specific idea until the actual launch is visible.
ideas.onScreenTitle: “Google just showed the part of AI normal people will use”
ideas.spokenHook: “Google I/O is today, and this is the one AI update I would actually pay attention to.”
ideas.why: It lets Braydon be early without forcing a weak news take. The bar is simple: only shoot if there is a visible demo or operator use case.

competitors.creator: digitalsamaritan
competitors.text: 3 months ago, I refused to make plans for the May long weekend and even declined a brand event for May 19th because Google announced I/O 2026 in Mountain View, California. At the time, I had no idea if I would ever get invited or if Google even invited creators. But when the @googlegemini team started commenting on some of my videos on Instagram and TikTok, I took it as a sign to shoot my shot. I emailed every contact I had, asking if they had any creator programs and pitching myself for an invite to this event. Long story short, guess who got an official invite to go to Google I/O in California next week? I grew up watching big tech YouTubers going to these events and never in a million years imagined that one day I would be one of them. Feel surreal!

competitors.topic: The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill
competitors.onScreenTitle: The Best Claude Memory Fix With the best Claude Handoff Skill
competitors.spokenHook: Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix so in thropic the creators of Claude advise that after 20 or 30 messages with Claude you start a brand new conversation but the problem is when you transfer to a new conversation sometimes Claude leaves out important details and so I I created a new skill called the handoff skill that makes sure that Claude captures all the important information and seamlessly transfers it to any new conversation and this handoff skill generates the summary using the exact format below and it's able to capture the core contexts key decisions what's pending and the tone and style of that conversation and then what it does is that it creates this beautiful hand off summary that it can feed directly inside of the next Claude conversation so now whenever you have a really long conversation inside of Claude all you need to do is type in this skill forward slash hand off and it will generate this perfect hand off summary that you can then copy and put into a brand new conversation with Claude to ensure that you start exactly where you left off without losing any context at all so if you want this exact handoff skill and a full guide on the best Claude skills and how to optimize your Claude setup just comment the word Claude and I'll go ahead and send it over to you and as always if you like this sort Of content and you want to stay ahead in the world of AI, make sure that you give me a follow.

competitors.creator: taki.gpt
competitors.text: Comment “CLAUDE” to get the best Claude optimization and setup guide on the internet. Boris Cherny created Claude Code and he just shared 10 tips on how he uses it. Here are the key takeaways. First, always start with Plan Mode. Do this before any code. Just press Shift+Tab to turn it on. Iterate on your plan. Make it perfect. This stops many errors later. Think of it like drawing a map first. Second, run multiple Claude Code sessions. Use each for a different project part. Then merge them at the end. Let AI do the merge. You finish projects three to five times faster. It’s like having extra hands working at once. Third, the agent makes a mistake? Tell it to update its own claude.md file. Do this every time. It learns. The same mistake won’t happen again. Simple as fixing a recipe note. Fourth, build sub-agents. Use them to automate your work. One agent writes code. Another reviews it. A third deploys it. They all run on their own. Picture a team passing a baton smoothly. The good news? These tips fit any workflow. Because anytime Claude helps with one part, it speeds up the rest: #borischerny #claudeai #claudecode #claudetips #aitipsandtricks
competitors.topic: 10 Pro Tips for Claude Code / From the creator of Claude Code!
competitors.onScreenTitle: 10 Pro Tips for Claude Code / From the creator of Claude Code!
competitors.spokenHook: The creator of Claude Code Boris Cherny just dropped 10 tips on how he uses Claude Code and here are the key takeaways I've personally tried all of them and I'll show you the top five of his tips if you want to get the most out of Claude and stay till the end because the last one is definitely the most impactful and if you like this sort of content and you want to get the most out of your a I experience make sure that you give me a follow tip number one is that he runs multiple Claude agents in parallel inside of his terminal and inside of the Claude desktop app it self and running multiple agents is useful because you can have each agent take care of specific responsibilities like one for coding one for testing one for design one for iteration and one for review tip No. 2 is he uses a shared Claude MD file which is basically a context file that teaches Claude everything about who he is, how he gets work done, his business etc and when you share that with your entire team everybody then has access to that same context so the results that they're gonna create are gonna be similar tip No.

briefMarkdown:
*Generated: May 19, 2026 at 6:49 AM MT*

# TikTok Ideas, May 19, 2026

**Strategist read.** Post from the lane that is already working: plainspoken Claude demos that show an obvious business outcome on screen. Braydon’s latest scrape says the same thing again. “Claude now makes cartoon videos” is at 199.7K views and “Claude is the best AI tool” is at 188.9K, while the new Claude small-business agent post is already at 12.1K after less than a day. The safest play today is not another abstract agent take. It is: show a business owner exactly what the agent does, then give them the first useful workflow.

## Performance Check-In

- **Top current Braydon post:** “Claude now makes cartoon videos” at 199.7K views, 8.6K likes, 1,981 shares. This is still the clearest proof that visual-output demos beat theory.
- **Second strongest current post:** “Claude is the best AI tool” at 188.9K views and 1,818 shares. The pattern is simple: show the output first, then explain why it matters.
- **New post to watch:** “Claude’s new agent for small business = 🔥🔥🔥” is at 12.1K views with 161 shares from May 18 evening. The save count came back as 0 in this scrape, so treat views/shares as the early signal.
- **New weaker post:** “Codex can now build web based video games 🔥🤯” is at 2.45K views. Codex still needs a broader business wrapper to travel beyond developer curiosity.
- **Canva signal:** Braydon’s “Claude can now connect to Canva” is at 9.29K views. Sabrina’s Claude + Canva posts are still live at 58.1K and 31.9K views, so this remains worth one more business-output angle if it is not a repeat.

## Idea 1: The Claude Small-Business Agent Actually Doing the Work

**Tag:** `🌊 wave-ride` `🔁 V2/reframe`

- **What it’s about:** Turn yesterday’s Claude small-business agent post into a concrete operator demo. Pick one tiny business task, like replying to leads, turning a messy note into an invoice email, or making a follow-up checklist, and show Claude doing the first draft.
- **Evidence:** Braydon’s May 18 Claude small-business agent post is already at 12.1K views and 161 shares. That is the strongest new own-account signal from the current scrape.
- **On-screen title:** “I gave Claude one boring business task”
- **Spoken hook:** “This is what Claude’s new small-business agent actually does when you give it a real job.”
- **Content type:** tutorial
- **Why this should work:** The audience already responded to the announcement. The V2 earns its slot by moving from “new agent exists” to “here is the task it can actually take off your plate.”

## Idea 2: Ask Better Finance Questions Before You Call Your Accountant

**Tag:** `📰 news` `🌊 wave-ride`

- **What it’s about:** Show the safe, practical version of the ChatGPT finance update: not “AI replaces your accountant,” but “AI helps you understand your numbers before the meeting.”
- **Evidence:** ChatGPTapp posted the finance preview on May 16, and the earlier finance/accounting competitor signal from nocode.joshua remains a high-performing category signal. The current X scan also showed Sam Altman carrying the ChatGPT product momentum forward on May 18.
- **On-screen title:** “Before you call your accountant, ask ChatGPT this”
- **Spoken hook:** “Do not let AI make your money decisions. But it can help you ask way better questions.”
- **Content type:** quick tip
- **Why this should work:** It gives Frank a clear use case in 5 seconds and avoids the replacement framing. It also ties a real OpenAI product update to a normal business owner problem.

## Idea 3: Claude Plus Canva, But Make It a Client Deliverable

**Tag:** `🌊 wave-ride` `🔁 V2/reframe`

- **What it’s about:** Show Claude + Canva making one real deliverable a small business owner would recognize: a one-page flyer, menu special, event promo, realtor listing sheet, or keynote announcement graphic.
- **Evidence:** Sabrina has two current Claude + Canva posts at 58.1K and 31.9K views. Braydon’s own Canva post is at 9.29K views. The source pattern is live, but Braydon’s angle should be more practical and less “Canva got replaced.”
- **On-screen title:** “Claude made this client flyer in Canva”
- **Spoken hook:** “If you run a small business, this is the part of AI that actually saves you time.”
- **Content type:** tool breakdown
- **Why this should work:** It borrows the live Canva wave without copying the competitor framing. Braydon’s distinct angle is the business deliverable, not the tool drama.

## Idea 4: Google I/O Watchlist, But Only If the Demo Is Visual

**Tag:** `📰 news`

- **What it’s about:** Google I/O is today. If Google drops a visual Gemini feature, a business workflow, or a new AI Studio/Gemini app capability, make a fast “what this means for normal people” video. If the announcement is mostly model benchmarks, skip it.
- **Evidence:** GoogleDeepMind posted on May 18 that Google I/O begins May 19 at 10 AM PT with AI breakthroughs and tools. This is fresh, but it has not earned a specific idea until the actual launch is visible.
- **On-screen title:** “Google just showed the part of AI normal people will use”
- **Spoken hook:** “Google I/O is today, and this is the one AI update I would actually pay attention to.”
- **Content type:** news
- **Why this should work:** It lets Braydon be early without forcing a weak news take. The bar is simple: only shoot if there is a visible demo or operator use case.

## Today's Posting Order

1. **Claude small-business agent actually doing the work.** Best mix of fresh own-account evidence and Braydon fit.
2. **Finance questions before your accountant.** Strong Frank-level clarity and current OpenAI product/news momentum.
3. **Claude + Canva client deliverable.** Good backup if Braydon can show the output quickly.
4. **Google I/O watchlist.** Only post after the keynote if the announcement gives a visual demo or obvious business workflow.

## Why these ideas and not something else

- I am not leading with Codex mobile or Codex games because Braydon’s latest Codex posts are still underperforming compared with Claude visual-output posts.
- I am not copying the competitor “Claude replaced Canva/Figma” framing because Braydon’s stronger lane is mentoring business owners through practical use cases.
- I am not turning Anthropic’s Stainless acquisition into a top idea. It is real news, but it is too backend/MCP-heavy for a general TikTok unless Braydon wants a credibility piece for developers and bookers.
- I am keeping Google I/O as conditional because the event is fresh, but the specific content angle needs the actual launch, not a teaser.

## Research Notes

### Competitors

- **Sabrina Ramonov:** Claude + Canva remains the clearest live competitor wave. Current scrape showed 58.1K views on “Claude x Canva = $10k / month 🤯” and 31.9K views on “Claude + Canva is a literal cheatcode.” Both had title and spoken-hook coverage in cache.
- **Marcin Teodoru:** A May 17 Anthropic fellowship post is at 184.1K views with strong comments and shares. The lesson is not to pitch the fellowship itself. The lesson is that “regular people can get access to frontier AI opportunities” travels when the benefit is concrete.
- **BrandNat:** Recent one-person-company framing is still a strong operator pattern. Her May 12 post is at 282.1K views, but it is older than today’s freshest wave.
- **Rachel Woods:** Current posts are lower-view but useful context: “expert in the loop” and AI operations are good language patterns for business owners.
- **Tony Aube and Nocode Joshua:** The scrape returned older or stale items for these handles, so I treated them as reference/context rather than current source evidence.

### News and early-catch

- Google I/O starts today at 10 AM PT. Fresh but conditional until the keynote produces a specific demo.
- Anthropic announced it is acquiring Stainless, an SDK and MCP server platform, on May 18. Good credibility signal, not a top TikTok unless simplified.
- ChatGPT product momentum is still active: finance preview, Codex mobile, and Sam Altman saying ChatGPT got much better with the latest update.

### Own performance

- Braydon’s best current content is still Claude visual output with plain “watch this” delivery.
- Claude small-business agent is the best new V2 candidate because it has early traction and a clear business owner promise.
- Codex needs a stronger visible business outcome before it deserves another top slot.

## Bullet-Point Scripts

### Idea 1: The Claude Small-Business Agent Actually Doing the Work

- Open with the task on screen: “reply to a new customer lead and make a follow-up checklist.”
- Say: “This is what Claude’s new small-business agent actually does when you give it a real job.”
- Show the messy input: a customer DM, rough notes, or a fake inquiry.
- Show Claude turning it into a reply, next steps, and a simple reminder/checklist.
- Explain in plain English: “It is not replacing you. It is taking the first draft off your plate.”
- Close with: “Start with one boring task, not your whole business.”

### Idea 2: Ask Better Finance Questions Before You Call Your Accountant

- Open with: “Do not let AI make your money decisions.”
- Show a simple prompt: “Here are my numbers. What should I ask my accountant?”
- Give three example questions it might produce: cash flow, expenses, tax planning.
- Clarify: “This is not financial advice. It is meeting prep.”
- Tie it to normal people: “You walk into the conversation less confused.”
- Close with: “AI is best when it helps you work better with experts.”

### Idea 3: Claude Plus Canva, But Make It a Client Deliverable

- Open with the finished flyer or promo graphic first.
- Say: “Claude and Canva can now make something a real client would actually use.”
- Show Claude creating the brief or copy.
- Show Canva turning it into the visual.
- Point out the business use: realtor listing, menu special, event promo, keynote graphic.
- Close with: “The win is not replacing designers. It is getting a usable first version fast.”

## Sources

- Braydon scrape artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-19-0641-cron-run/braydon_compact.json`
- Competitor scrape artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-19-0641-cron-run/competitors_compact.json`
- Run summary: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-19-0641-cron-run/summary.json`
- Hook cache: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/tiktok-hooks-cache.json`
- Performance log: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/tiktok-performance-log.md`
- Early-catch log: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/early-catch-log.md`
- X scan included GoogleDeepMind, AnthropicAI, ChatGPTapp, OpenAI/OpenAIDevs, Sam Altman, and other high-signal AI accounts.

## Run notes and limitations

- Competitor scrape returned 50 posts across all 10 configured handles.
- New competitor posts found: 6.
- New-post on-screen title coverage: 3/6.
- New-post spoken-hook coverage: 3/6.
- New-post complete title + hook coverage: 3/6.
- Recent 7-10 day competitor-window coverage: 29/32 titles, 27/32 spoken hooks, 27/32 both.
- Three new recent competitor posts came from inline MCP output where metrics/captions were visible but asset URLs were not fully mirrored into the local `/tmp` artifacts. I preserved them as caption/performance signals and did not call them hook signals.
- Braydon’s two newest posts had cover/title extraction attempted. Both were missing subtitle links in the scrape, so spoken hooks are marked `—` rather than guessed.
- No `NEEDS_VISION_OCR` placeholders remain in the hook cache.
